L1.area: Database schema introspection and SQL query construction layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a crafted identifier is interpolated directly into SQL query strings used by database metadata retrieval operations without proper escaping of special characters.

L3.area: Table metadata loading logic and SQL identifier quoting utilities
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where table name identifiers are interpolated directly into SQL query strings used for retrieving column and index metadata from the database, without escaping backtick characters that serve as identifier delimiters in MySQL.

ground_truth.reason: Table names are directly concatenated into SQL query strings in database metadata retrieval functions without escaping backtick characters, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL via a crafted table name.
